title: Conversion without escape: Caste beyond religion and the limits of equality
description: The Supreme Court’s recent decision in Chinthada Anand v. State of Andhra Pradesh presents itself as an exercise in doctrinal fidelity, but on closer inspection, it reveals a deeper constitutional unease. By holding that conversion to a religion outside Hinduism, Sikhism or Buddhism results in the immediate and complete loss of scheduled caste status, the Court relies squarely on Clause 3 of the Constitution (Scheduled Castes) Order, 1950.The clarity of this rule is undeniable.
